name: Bi-Omstilling Maridalen
description: Bi-Omstilling (Bi-O) is working for transition within areas defined by how far bees reach from their hive. (Bi=Bee, Omstilling=Transition) We are operating an organic garden project in Maridalen, a cultural heritage landscape north of Oslo which also contains the Maridalen lake, which is serving the city of Oslo with 85% of its drinking water. We will start beekeeping from the 2024 season, and at the same time invite the appr. 1000 inhabitants living within the area to participate in creating a carbon negative society within 2030. We will also work to encourage the local farmers and gardeners to go all organic and increase local food production. Since the project site is within 20 min. by bus from Oslo, with 600 000 inhabitants, we believe it can have a more general impact as well. *** Bi-Omstilling (Bi-O) jobber for omstilling innenfor områder definert av hvor langt biene når fra bikuben. Vi driver et økologisk hageprosjekt i Maridalen, et kulturminnelandskap nord for Oslo som også inneholder Maridalsvannet, som betjener Oslo by med 85 % av sitt drikkevann. Vi starter birøkt fra sesongen 2024, og inviterer samtidig de ca. 1000 innbyggere som bor i området for å være med på å skape et karbonnegativt samfunn innen 2030. Vi vil også jobbe for å oppmuntre de lokale bøndene og hagedyrkerne til å dyrke økologisk og øke lokal matproduksjon. Siden prosjektet er innen 20 min. med buss fra Oslo, med 600 000 innbyggere, tror vi det også kan ha en mer generell innvirkning.

name: Omstilling Sagene (Oslo, Norge)
description: Sagene is an urban district in the centre of Oslo with approximately 35,000 inhabitants. Our group started in November 2010 and has since implemented a number of projects in the community. Here's a brief list of what our small group has managed to carry out with a tiny budget and lots of enthusiasm! annual bike repair workshop (donations given to an ecological orphanage in Thailand) "exchange markets" (byttemarked på norsk) storytelling course the Garden Trailer - challenging the street parking paradigm in Oslo the "Wish Tree" - local democracy in action! permaculture park gardening soil building project/local food production movie screenings with guest speakers and small group discussions vermiculture course sour dough bread baking course homebrewing course edible weeds in the city walk/cooking course lactic acid fermentation course gardening at kindergardens information displays - raising awareness about Omstilling Sagene and the Transition movement seed bomb stand permaculture dance workshop fruit press and jucing stand on Park(ing) Day with local pears and apples from the parks LETS.no - Norway's first local exchange trading system herbal butter demonstration stand jam and chutney course Friendship North/South cooperation with Ecobarrio Villa4Alamos inner Transition cabin trip (hytte tur på norsk) seminar about the "New Economy" in cooperation with Grønn Hverdag presentations and support for neighboring Transition Initiatives All of our courses are offered free of char...

name: Grey Lynn 2030
description: Grey Lynn 2030 - Getting Started Founded in 2008, Grey Lynn 2030 was established by people with keeness for action and readiness to grow a Transition based reslient suburb in the heart of Tamaki Makaurau/Auckand. With engagement from locals, many who are still involved, Grey Lynn 2030 formed. In 2008, Grey Lynn 2030 had visions of being reslient, being able to cater for our own needs, rely less on oil and gas, imported products and lower our climate emissions. All our initiatives and projects since 2008 are true to this vision. At heart, Grey Lynn 2030 is a community-led group promoting and engaging in building neighbourhood spirit and cooperation while working towards a vision of a self-reliant, positive, resilient, vibrant, connected and sustainable community./
tags: Transition Group

name: Richmond Community Garden / Riverlution
description: We support the local community by teaching self sufficiency through growing veggies while having fun. We are a pioneer organisation leading the charge in food resilience, ecological restoration and community engagement in the Red Zone. The Riverlution Eco Hub is a sustainability and environmental hub situated in the Richmond Community Garden, 46A Vogel Street 🌱
tags: Transition Group, Community Growing, Nature, Community hub or activities, Festivals, fairs or events, Share, Repair or reuse

name: Transition Matamata
description: Transition Matamata is a non-profit community group promoting energy conservation, sustainable living, and a strong and resilient local economy. Established in 2013, the group strives to cater to local interests and serve community needs. Initiatives include how-to workshops, film nights, and special events and activities. An eco home tour, featuring a local home with many ‘green' features, drew a crowd - many attendees looking for ideas for their own renovation or building project. Another one involved a ‘road trip' to tour and inspection several homes with different types of sustainable construction. Support is provided to schools establishing vege gardens on their grounds and the ‘Matamata bee Fruitful' project is planting fruit trees on public land. The group works with the local council to encourage their sustainability efforts, including recycling/resource recovery and active/sustainable transportation. Those wishing to receive Transition Matamata's e-newsletter should send their email address to info@aquas.co.nz
tags: Transition Group

name: Transition Town Kaitaia
description: Transition Town Kaitāia was established in Far North of Aotearoa/NZ in 2009, met monthly until 2019, and was instrumental in setting up Kaitāia Timebank, a local Savings Pool, Māra Kai Community Garden, and EcoCentre - which are all still active! TTK no longer formally meets but has a monthly newsletter with 200+ readership. Former TTK members are active across a range of new initiatives: Kaitāia Cycle (food-waste collection & composting), Anō Anō (clothing & resource repurposing), Repair Cafe, Plastic-Free Kaitāia, etc.

name: Aldeia das Amoreiras Sustentável
description: The project Aldeia das Amoreiras Sustentável (sustainable village of Amoreiras) is an holistic local development project aimed at creating a sustainable village through the participation and empowerment of the local community. The strategy of the coordination group Centro de Convergencia (Convergence Centre, see www.centrodeconvergencia.org) was to make several meetings in the streets asking what is the dream village or what are the dreams that the people have for their village. The result was the concept of a sustainable village, the vision. See the movie in http://vimeo.com/16109018 We are now joining the people in groups to work around the dreams and make them reality. We are also making a Permaculture design to the whole village based on the dreams and a detailled questionary applied to all the 200 inhabitants. The Permaculture solutions and design will be appropriated by the local working groups and the ecological and integrated solutions will be implemented by the population. The Convergence Centre is acting as a animator, facilitator and organisator at the same time. We have also invited a group of external facilitators for all the group meetings. We have invited all possible organisations in the region to be partners. In the coordinating group there are 6 people with Permaculture Design Course and two people with the Transition Initiative Course. Our challenge is to integrate the landscape around the village and private property in the process of managing for the c...

name: Cooperativa Integral da Terra, CRL
description: Cooperativa Integral da Terra is a multi-sector cooperative based in Aljezur, Portugal, creating a regenerative and resilient local economy. It integrates small producers, artisans, educators, and cultural initiatives through shared legal, administrative, and financial structures. Guided by transparency, cooperation, and community wellbeing, it enables members to develop independent projects in agriculture, craftsmanship, education, housing, and culture, promoting collective abundance and social regeneration.

name: Famalicão em Transição
description: We are a group of citizens from Vila Nova de Famalicão, a municipality north of Portugal, committed to contribute actively to make our community more sustainable and resilient. To do this, we progressively change our behaviors towards a more sustainable life, less consumerist, and in harmony with nature and the community. We are building awareness on this matters and new and positive stories between our community. Our principles and methodology are inspired by the Transition Network. We joined for the first time in the LIMPAR PORTUGAL initiative in 2010 (CLEAN PORTUGAL), and since then we have been promoting various activities and events. The initial transition group was assumed at the meeting on July 1, 2011, and, on August 25, 2016, we created an association, "Associação Famalicão em Transição". We hope to continue and to expand our activities for a more sustainable future. We have several working groups: Inner Transition, Education, Circular Economy, Enviromnment and Comunication. See what we aim and what we do in our blogue

name: HortaFCUL
description: The HortaFCUL project was created at the Faculty of Sciences of the Lisbon University in 2009, led by a group of students interested in the topic of permaculture and how it could provide a solution to some of the current ecological, social and economic problems. With the support of the college board and the dedication of some volunteers, HortaFCUL is a permanent food garden next to the C2 building, a space that connects agriculture to gardening, integrates a constantly changing social dynamic, a space that is considered the heart of the project. This project emerged with the aim of raising awareness and demonstrating more ecological practices, based on the ethics, principles, strategies, techniques and tools proposed by permaculture.​​​​​​​​​ It aims to enhance the campus' ecosystem services and, thus, contribute to the pursuit of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

name: Paredes em Transição
description: Paredes em Transição is a grassroots’ movement that was born in the town of Paredes, in Northern Portugal. It focuses on building resilience, on the re-localization of the economy and on turning it less dependent on fossil fuels. Our most recent event was the public screening of Yann Arthus-Bertrand's documentary film Home, in coordination with our friends from Portalegre em Transição. Our last activity was a re-skilling workshop on eco-soapmaking, and we keep on exchanging seeds seedlings & produce. A couple of permablitzen have occurred. Other food-related adventures include elder flower cordial, bread, yoghurt, cheese, jam and other preserves making. Future events will include a seminary on issues like Peak Oil, escalade on fossil fuel & food prices, climate changes, economic contraction & the Transition model, and more screenings of relevant films and documentaries, open space events, a local farmers and producers' market and an allotment program. An official website is in the making. We've just become a formal organization. *********** O movimento Paredes em Transição nasceu da vontade de um grupo de cidadãos de Paredes em tornar a sua comunidade mais resiliente e sustentável, menos dependente em energias fósseis e do abastecimento de alimentos longínquos. Recentemente tivemos uma exibição do filme do fotógrafo francês Yann Arthus-Bertrand, Home. O Mundo é a Nossa Casa, em simultâneo com os nossos colegas de Portalegre. Eventos futuros contarão com um c...

name: origens - como tudo começa...
description: O Projeto "origens - como tudo começa…", parte de uma iniciativa privada, numa área com cerca de 9 ha, localizados na Freguesia de Orgens em Viseu. Orgens é uma freguesia periurbana, muito próxima do centro da cidade, mas que encerra em si um ecossistema único, de elevado valor ambiental e patrimonial. Este projeto teve como primeiro impulsionador Humberto Mendes, licenciado em Ciências da Arquitectura, um ativista local das questões sociais e ambientais. Anteriormente estudou o território do Parque Natural de Montesinho, as suas aldeias e gentes, e o seu passado de organização comunitária. Desde esse período, há mais de 10 anos, num trabalho conjunto e esforço familiar, tem tentado criar condições para que seja possível, a criação de uma comunidade, num género de partilha coletiva, num conceito de pesquisa, de reflexão e debate para que sejam propostas e colocadas em prática "novas" (provavelmente ancestrais) formas de nos relacionarmos enquanto espécie e entre espécies, num ecossistema local de características próprias. Essa comunidade parte de princípios como partilha, democracia participativa, agroecologia, agrofloresta, conservação e regeneração de ecossistemas, em total respeito pela nossa Mãe Terra. Não é este um projeto fácil, nem o seu caminho será curto, num mundo acelerado e concentrado noutras formas de vida e de estar, ainda para mais a ser proposto numa área tão próxima e apetecível do ponto de vista urbanístico. Estamos em crer que com a vontade coleti...

description: Bucuresti in Transitie started in January 2012 (from the 1st national permaculture gathering that took place in the summer of 2011) through a introductory workshop in Transition. Since then we have organized different activities in which we engaged beautiful people that soon became an informal community of people interested in permaculture, transition, low impact living, alternative living and thinking. What we have done so far is: movies screenings for "In Transition 1.0" and In "Transition 2.0" public presentations of Transition Towns concepts organized events and activities dedicated to promotion of permaculture (we are proud to have organized in Bucharest an introductory workshop in Permaculture with Rod Everett and Mill Millichap in the fall of 2012), Community Supported Agriculture, etc. developed projects (that are in different stages of development) oriented towards susteinability and community, which are: creating a seed bank school gardening informat permaculture school/meeting groups urban/recicled art textile recycling (sowing learning project) cooking club We have started to develop specialized teams of people that are working on growing Transition Towns in Bucharest/Romania: communication team, events team, IT/web design-development team, projects team, documentation/translations team. name: Omställning Alingsås
description: We formed a stearing group 3 november 2009. We have a local home page att http://www.omstallningalingsas.seWe are meeting on a local café every monday evening. We have several workinggroups. Every Thursday our bike kitchen is open for anyone that wants to come and mend their bike. On Sundays the gardening group meets on the piece of land that we borrow from the municipality in the city park. On Saturdays during the summer we arrange a flea-market in town for anyone to come without cost to sell what they do not need. Our heart and soul group meet more irregulary and the core group meets every month. We arrange a future week in march together with other organisations and the municipality. 2014 there where 120 events in Alingsås and the sourrounding country-side during one week. Today we have 14 people that have done Transition Launch, Two people who have attended Transition Thrive and Two persons that have gone through The Train The Trainers course. On top of that we have three persons who have completed the Permaculture course of 72 hours.

description: We are an up and coming transition at Gessayova, Bratislava. We are in the process of starting a new community garden, while continually getting to know our neighbours. So far so good. The initiative has been started and will hopefully remain quite open to everyobody form the neigbourhood, and also to their ideas for the community and the public space we have decided to legally occupy. To community, consensus and root vegetables!

name: EcoThailand Foundation
description: About Us EcoThailand (นิเวศวิถี) is a not for profit NGO, with Thai registered Charitable Foundation Status, based in Surat Thani province Thailand. We have a strong Environmental and Community focus and are active on Koh Phangan, Koh Samui, nearby Gulf Islands, and the provincial Southern Thai mainland, including Surat Thani, Nakhon si Thammarat and Phuket. Caring for our Environment We endeavor to promote environmental sustainability, education, awareness, guardianship and direct action among children, youth, local adult groups and businesses in the Thai Gulf through the promotion and facilitation of co-working, co-ordination and networking, between ourselves and partners, to achieve locally agreed goals for the benefit of the local environment and all of its diverse communities. Supporting Education in Schools and Communities We believe in the power of change and that the best gift we can give to future generations, the most valuable legacy we can leave behind us, is to help empower students to be leaders and guardians for sustainability and positive change, by enhancing local education …. Engaging the youth of today to protect the planet of tomorrow .Bringing communities together and supporting great ideas. Bringing communities together and supporting great ideas Community foundations come in all shapes and sizes, but what we all have in a common is a commitment to making a difference to people’s lives and the places they live. name: Urban Blossom
description: Urban Blossom hopes to become a bridge connecting urban residents with the countryside, agriculture, the land, and mother nature. We are a group of partners based in Taichung. Since 2024, we have believed that ecological environments can also be cultivated within cities, and that urban areas can nurture their own place-based ecological communities. Therefore, we began by creating soil, promoting household food-waste composting solutions in the city, practicing urban farming that learns to “dialogue with nature,” and encouraging diets based on friendly, responsibly grown crops. Through a “half-farmer, half-X” lifestyle , we encourage people to practice and prepare for living in greater harmony with nature. Starting in 2026, we will engage in Transition Town practices such as open-space workshops and reading groups. Through these activities, we hope to connect with more partners who are already taking similar actions, and to co-create a diverse urban community network shaped by a rich ecological ecosystem. In this way, people living in cities can support and connect with one another, becoming a collective force moving toward a more Earth-friendly way of life.

name: Kasese Platform Advocacy Network (KAPLANET)
description: Kasese Platform Advocacy Network (KAPLANET) is a dynamic, youth-led grassroots organization committed to strengthening social support systems by promoting social justice, health equity, and inclusive youth empowerment. Our mandate focuses on building an inclusive youth movement that harnesses collective synergies to foster sustainable peace, non-violence, fairness and respect for human rights in communities affected by conflict. Through innovative, holistic, and creative strategies designed by youth, for youth, and with youth , KAPLANET unlocks the transformative potential of young people as catalysts for lasting positive change and generational impact.

name: Permaculture Initiative Uganda
description: Permaculture Initiative Uganda aims to bring about peace via affordable permaculture education. Since 2017 , Tebandeke Ali, has worked to educate and empower with the help of non profit organizations including Mwebaza Foundation, Sector39, Alliance For Africa's Orphanages , The Norwegian Refugee Council , Oxfam South Africa and the Kampala Capital City Authority . Our passion is to create resilient communities while helping them meet their own human and environmental needs.

name: Regenar Development Initiative
description: The Regenar Development Initiative (RDI) is a not-for-profit organization based in Kampala, Uganda, dedicated to promoting Regenerative Development and Design. RDI works to convene communities to regenerate their biocultural heritage and foster economic vitality while co-evolving with the natural ecosystems in their place. The organization focuses on creating regenerative systems that align human activity with the health of the environment, culture, and community. RDI’s flagship initiative is the Kiwaatule-2030 project, which aims to restore and regenerate the Nalubaaga wetland ecosystem while rebuilding housing, commercial, and community spaces in the Kiwaatule suburb of Kampala. By 2030, the project aims to transform the area into a regenerative suburb, fostering ecological, social, cultural, and economic health. The initiative is centered around the Nalubaaga Valley Community (NVC), a place-owned, collectively governed, distributive cooperative organization. The NVC is designed to steward the health of both people and natural ecosystems, particularly around the Nalubaaga wetland. The wetland, which flows through Kiwaatule, Kigoowa, and Najjeera, is part of a network of over 30 wetlands in Kampala that are under threat due to urban sprawl and environmental degradation. The name Nalubaaga Valley Community reflects the belief that by reconnecting to and centering the health of the Nalubaaga wetland/stream's natural flow, the community can sustainably nurture social, natura...

name: Rich & Safe Foods Group ( R&SF)
description: R & SF Group is a petty,infant transition group whose major aim is, "to re-engine the income growth rate as well as welbeing of households". It is an agribusiness firm with motto, "Start Small, Grow Big". Here, we majorly focus on growth of crops (mainly recognized as food crops) that are readily available to locals, of which sweet potatoes have emerged as our leading crop. Globally, our end results are: to ensure food security, act as a source of knowledge on sustainable agriculture, and ensure reconomization of available resources so as to positively effect the economy.

name: Umbrella for Journalists in Kasese
description: Umbrella for Journalists in Kasese (UJK) is an indigenous organisation that is working towards enhancing freedom of expression, safety of Journalists and amplifying community voices in Rwenzori Sub Region. UJK uses community reporting and storytelling as an approach towards supporting local voices for Sustainable Community Led Development, Capacity Building, Research and Advocacy to Strengthen Accountability and Open governance in areas of Human Rights, Climate Justice and Livelihood Improvement. Core Values Integrity: To be honesty and reliable to our values Teamwork: Believing in a collective effort to serve our community. Accountability: To be accountable for our actions to all stakeholders. Equity: To be fair and just to everyone without discrimination.

name: Amherst
description: Transition Amherst raises awareness of the challenges we face due to climate change, peak oil and economic instability. We work to build local resilience by 1) encouraging skill-sharing and projects that lead to greater sustainability, 2) connecting local groups and people, and 3) bringing together our community's resources and creativity to craft an inclusive path to a lower energy future.

name: Bolton Local
description: Bolton Local is a grassroots group of people from Bolton, Massachusetts, whose mission is to build a strong, self-reliant community culture made up of people committed to living sustainably on the planet, in a way that inspires, builds friendships, and offers a hopeful vision for the future.
tags: Transition Group

name: Braided Relations
description: Braided Relations recenters our Indigenous, spiritual, artistic and ancestral practices as paths to shared liberation. We recognize that colonialism—its structure of capitalism and its predecessor of imperialism—creates intersecting forms of oppression, distancing us from our ancestral knowledge and causing a schism between us, the land and her collective sentience. Braided Relations brings together practitioners and the public to share rituals, experience spiritual and cultural (re)awakening, and reconnect with ourselves, each other, the earth and all her kin so that we can build the power needed to liberate all beings.

name: Carey Link
description: Carey Link is dedicated to building a stronger, more resilient community in our village. We aim to build awareness about sustainability issues and create new opportunities for people to work, play, and flourish locally. Since our inception in 2014, we've hosted monthly talked and documentaries, created a community garden, started a farmers market, built a new nature-focused park in cooperation with our village council, hosted monthly repair clinics, and held numerous events centering on local food and other issues. We're eager to expand the positive impact we can have on our community, and to help other communities do the same. Please contact us at our email address listed below with questions or to sign up for our monthly newsletter. Check us out below on Facebook or Twitter. Or, better yet, stop in!
tags: Transition Group, Community Growing, Other food projects, Nature, Community hub or activities, Art / creativity, Youth or education projects, Share, Repair or reuse

name: Charlotte
description: We have been hosting a series of re-skilling workshops on canning and preserving food, keeping poultry, keeping goats, sharpening tools, health care in the transition, home composting, and unplugging with children. We have trained "home energy visitors" (not audits) where neighbors help neighbors reduce their energy use. The energy committee has completed an audit of town buildings and major renovations are underway at the Town Hall.
tags: Transition Group

name: Chippewa Valley/Eau Claire WI Transitioning Group
description: Chippewa Valley/Eau Claire Wisconsin Transitioning Group is a group of area residents from the cities of Eau Claire, Chippewa Falls, and rural Eau Claire, Dunn and Chippewa Counties who joined together following a discussion course held at our public library. The discussion course followed a study guide based on Rob Hopkins' Transition Handbook. Our group is currently working on general vision and mission statements. The Chippewa Valley Transitioning Group is made up of the following sub-groups: Affordable Solar, Awareness, East Side Neighborhood, Climate Action Now (CAN), Fiber Arts, Youth Eco-Teams Education, Permaculture, and Community Greenhouse. Some of these groups are holding regular meetings twice a month. We are meeting every other month for a group pot-luck and education activity. Please contact our group for more information and for meeting times.
tags: Transition Group

name: Columbia Resilience
description: We achieved 501c3 status in July 2014. Our current working groups include Community Orchard Working Group and Community Integrative Health Working Group. Please visit us at www.columbiaresilience.org.

name: Community Harvest SRQ
description: Community Harvest SRQ (Transition Sarasota) was founded in 2010 as part of the international Transition Towns Movement. Community Harvest SRQ continues to play an integral role in building a thriving, people-powered community, which cultivates local food and economic security through our Suncoast Gleaning Project, Fruit Rescue Program, Farmers Market Produce Recovery, Eat Local Guide, and Annual Eat Local Week. True to our grassroots history, our work organizing educational programs, innovative projects, outreach, community action, and economic partnerships continues to consciously adapt and transform to what our community needs most.
tags: Transition Group, Other food projects, Festivals, fairs or events, Building local networks

name: Community ONE @ Sycamore Spring Farm
description: Sycamore Spring Farm began as a homestead farm almost 300 years ago. We are located in Frederick Md, just outside the city and very convenient to routes 70/270/340/15. 21 years ago we began a Local, sustainable, Beyond Organic Community Supported Agricultural (CSA) program as well as various educational workshops to teach, assist and support others in reaching their own sustainable, healthier, eco-friendly, Food Security and Community goals. We emphasize less dependence on fossil fuels and encourage the village concept of caring and sharing. We are hopeful to continue growing a larger footprint by networking with like-minded folk to continue the beneficial services and education that will spread from Farm Partner to Farm Partner and beyond into our local communities, as the GROWTH of these initiatives and ideas is very important for the future of all of us and our planet.

name: Design, Energy, and the Environment
description: I live in Lamar, MO. I feel that nobody in my community would join this, but I am sure there are people living in the greater Joplin area that are interested in the Transition.
tags: Transition Group

name: East Valley Transition
description: East Valley Transition is a group of residents of the East Valley in Phoenix, AZ interested in bringing about transition and localization to our neighborhoods and towns. Whether you are into gardening, composting, biking, canning, homeschooling or organizing community events, you are already practicing transition towards a greener, localized lifestyle. Please join the conversation and contribute your ideas on how to get a ‘Transition Town' started in the East Valley. WHAT IS A ‘TRANSITION TOWN'? ‘Transition' is a community based approach to become ‘resilient' - self sufficient and interdependent, creative and celebratory, ecologically mindful and socially interconnected. Due to globalization, we are at the end of the long supply chains providing basic products, goods and services. With this in mind, we are also disconnected from each other, and our economy is dependent on non-renewable fuels that degrade natural systems and deplete resources. Our communities are not resilient when faced with peak oil, climate change and economic turmoil. The Transition movement is a process for planned, immediate and voluntary energy descent by neighborhoods, towns and/or cities, envisioning the resulting future as positive and better. It is a social experiment on a grand scale, with 380+ official initiatives worldwide, including 90+ in the United States. List of official transition initiatives in the United States - http://transitionus.org/initiatives-map. If inter...
tags: Transition Group

name: Fidalgo & Friends
description: Transition Fidalgo & Friends had its origin back in 2006 when we were known as Skagit Beat the Heat, an all-volunteer group focusing on climate change issues. We've evolved into a transition initiative in order to expand our focus to include peak oil, as well as to shift to a more positive tone to engage more of the community, and to tie into a growing regional transition network. Visit our website to access our book Living Well, Living Green in Skagit and Whatcom Counties , find info on our local cookbook, Serving the Skagit Harvest , and learn about our upcoming talks, films, skill-share workshops and 7th Generation Suppers! We also encourage you to loin our online forum and share ideas about how we can build resilience and reduce energy on Fidalgo and Guemes Islands. Let's make our island home a place of low fossil-fuel energy, high community energy!
tags: Transition Group

name: Grass Valley Friends Meeting
description: The Grass Valley Friends Meeting (http://gvfriends.org/) is just entering the mulling stage. We have several people expressing interest, and are planning on bringing the Transition Town Initiatives to the attention of the rest of the members of Grass Valley Friends Meeting (Quakers). This is not (yet) an official initiative approved by GVFM, but is merely in the exploratory phase. The information listed here is to allow for ease of contact by similarly minded local or affiliated interested parties. We are associated, and co-located with, the Woolman School (http://woolman.org/), a nonprofit educational community dedicated to the principles of peace, justice and sustainability. We note with interest the APPLE Center Initiative in Nevada City. We are already aware of the APPLE Center, and as soon as we form a core group, and if we gain the approval of the Meeting to proceed, we will be contacting APPLE to discuss working together, joining, and/or supporting.
tags: Transition Group

name: Greensboro, NC
description: Beginning a transition initiative to address sustainability and resilience concerns in Greensboro, North Carolina, USA, such as food production systems, environmental health, community-building, local businesses and economy, waste-handling and recycling, non-petroleum based energy and transportation, and similar concerns. We're forming an initial steering committee and focusing on events that will lead up to our "unleashing."
tags: Transition Group

name: Grow Local Greenwood Lake
description: To create a regenerative , resilient, local food system to enhance the environmental and social health of Greenwood Lake NY
tags: Transition Group

name: Ithaca
description: Sustain and regenerate. There are so many amazing people and groups in the Ithaca area (including other parts of Tompkins county and beyond) the intentions for this group are to coordinate, collaborate, act and change. Coordinate - our knowledge, attitudes, skills, and aspirations to further our effectiveness Collaborate - for a truly resilient transition that is enacted by all of us, without a central leader or governing body Act - to work together, to bring our ideas to fruition, to be the change we want to see in the world Change - resilience requires the capacity to change, acclimate, and adapt. There will be many changes in the coming years, our communities need to be able to flex and absorb the impacts So far, I am mulling this over and decided to begin action-oriented next achievable steps. Please join me, this can't be done alone. Contact me at samuel(dot)bosco(at)gmail(dot)com
tags: Transition Group

name: Joshua Tree
description: Transition Joshua Tree — creating resilient and sustainable Mojave Desert Communities. Transition Joshua Tree — the 96th official Transition Town in the US, is a community-building network of individuals and affiliated projects and groups that work together in various ways to: 1) Encourage the citizens of the Morongo Basin to face and understand the growing threats to our community’s quality of life due to the interconnected challenges of climate change, an unstable global economy and the end of inexpensive and abundant fossil fuels 2) Foster a dynamic community organizing process that unleashes the collective genius of our citizens to envision a better future and start the transition to a more resilient, sustainable way of life. 3) Organize the community to develop a comprehensive Energy Action Plan that will delineate our transition away from fossil fuels. Towards these ends we organize educational community events that deal with the urgent subject of transitioning to a sustainable local infrastructure. This includes healthy food production, water conservation and water harvesting techniques, alternative building methods, transportation with a low carbon footprint and clean energy production.
tags: Transition Group

name: Kalamazoo
description: We have formed work groups around the topics of Waste (at this time elimination of plastic bags), Energy (around energy efficiency), Food, and Health and Wellness.
tags: Transition Group

name: Katonah, NY
description: This is a mulling group. I'm working to create an interest in Katonah to begin an official initiative here. With the support of our newly formed and official Transition Westchester hub, I'm hoping it will be a little easier getting the word out. I just need the support of my community. To find out more of what I and some of my peers have been doing check out our blog: TransitionKatonahBedford and our facebook pages here Transition Katonah/Bedford Facebook and Transition Westchester Right now, it's just me emailing my friends and neighbors updates on reskilling events, meetings and other happenings around the county. Pretty soon I hope that four more local, brave souls will step up and we can begin the real work of Transitioning Katonah! The beauty of Katonah is that it's already so close to being in Transition! With a few more tweaks it will be an incredibly resilient town to live and work in and ready to meet the uncertain challenges of the future. I am a NY state certified educator, a certified landscape designer from the NY Botanical Gardens, an artist and a life long gardener who has been studying and applying permaculture methods for many years. Local food security is high on my list of priorities. In the light of the recent wacky storms, extended power outages, and limited food supplies (trucks couldn't travel) I feel it's only logical for our community to address these issues now while we have the luxury of hindsight and resources, rather than wait for the next cris...
tags: Transition Group

name: Kirkland in Transition
description: Kirkland in Transition is part of a movement to envision and work towards a future of interdependence and cooperation. The Transition Town movement has developed a model for meeting the future as resilient, joyful communities. It envisions people working in their own towns to make their locale as diverse, resilient, and vibrant as possible, creating a local community that will grow more of its own food, generate more of its own power, build houses using local materials, and more (you can find more information about the Transition Town movement at transitionus.org). The Transition Town movement has a broad range of interests, including energy, food, transportation, economy, green building, and what it calls "reskilling," the bringing back of the skills that communities relied on a hundred years ago-not in a sentimental, nostalgic way, but looking forward to combine the best of new technology and the essential skills for living without relying on big corporations and fossil fuel for everything. This chapter is currently dormant, but its members are active in Indivisible Mohawk Valley, especially the Climate Crisis Working Group; Wild Ones Mohawk Valley (MV Lawns Gone Wild); Clinton/Kirkland Climate Smart Communities; Kirkland NY Trails; and other local groups.

name: Land of the Sky
description: (S) pecial Events – Production Support, Theme Parties, Cultural Events, Seminars (P) layshops – Fun forms of Individual and Group Guidance. (I) ntegrative Design – Multi-Disciplinary Team Approach. (R) eferral Programs – Global Community Human Resource Department. (A) rts Education – Serviced through a unique and talented network of teachers. (L) eadership Training – Motivational Exercises for Early Childhood to Adult. Spiral Enterprises is a Creative Clearinghouse for Arts and Education. Our bond is the passion to promote positive self-expression through the synergy of kinetic artistry. We build our bridges across age, musical, and cultural gaps in support of collective development. Our service is ushers for sensory invigoration, and emissaries in community education. We are honored to share with you, as we ignite your spirit and awaken your energy!

name: Local 20/20 of Port Townsend & Jefferson County
description: Our mission is working together toward local sustainability ~ integrating economy, ecology and community through action and education. Our action groups include:Citizens For Local PowerClimate ActionEmergency PreparednessFood Resiliency and Community GardensNorth Olympic Exchange (barter)Zero WasteTransportation Laband more If you live in Port Townsend, Washington, or the surrounding communities of Jefferson County, and want to get involved in creating a more sustainable future, please join us! The Local 20/20 steering committee meets monthly at the Food Co-op Annex in Port Townsend the fourth Tuesday of the month, 6:30-8:30 p.m. Newcomers are welcome. To join our weekly announcement email list, please email your request to local2020@l2020.org.
tags: Transition Group

name: Lopez Island
description: MISSION STATEMENT Transition Lopez Island is committed to engaging our community in transformational change for a livable future. In uncertain times, resilience is the capacity to bounce back and thrive. Our group is finding ways to harness our island-grown community activism and collective wisdom to create a new paradigm around issues of local economy, labor share, energy use, movement of goods and people, and food and water security that sustains life for us all. As part of a worldwide movement of community-scale initiatives, Transition Lopez Island also partners locally with like-minded groups, including the Lopez Community Land Trust, Lopez Locavores, Lopez Community Trails Network, San Juan Islands Agricultural Guild, Lopez Solid Waste, and the San Juan Islands Climate & Sustainability Advisory Committee. We invite our fellow islanders to join us in creating a bold future for our rock that promotes health, creativity, and equality for everyone, in balance with nature. Steering Committee Meetings are scheduled monthly to debrief past events, plan future activities, and make policy decisions for the larger group by consensus if possible. Anyone interested in joining the committee and playing a more central role in Transition Lopez Island's leadership is welcome to attend. In 2019, Transition Lopez Island launched our "Fossil Free by '33" campaign in which we organized into three teams: Food/Agriculture/Water asked "How can Lopez feed itself?" This team has sponsored "Ea...

name: Los Angeles (Westchester area)
description: The Environmental Change-Makers community group was founded in November 2005 to gather people around the ideas of environmental solutions — What We Can Do about our environmental problems. As we learned what Hopkins et al were doing in England, we began trying those ideas out too. Thus the Environmental Change-Makers became the "initiating group" for Transition action across much of the greater Los Angeles area. The launch of Transition Los Angeles in December 2008 meant a redefinition of the Environmental Change-Makers; where our events used to draw participants from all over the greater Los Angeles area, now those people join Transition action in their own local neighborhoods. The Environmental Change-Makers continue to gather, now focused on our local Westchester neighborhood. We maintain an active community garden, out of which we teach an Organic Vegetable Gardening class series. The Harvest Westchester local food redistribution program was a natural extension of the garden action, as were group purchases of fruit trees and rain barrels. We started a LETSystem (local bartering / community-based finances) for our local area. And we continue to host local Transition-focused gatherings in the Westchester neighborhood of Los Angeles. As of October 2010 we are at the stage of building a steering group which would be specific to our Westchester neighborhood.
tags: Transition Group

name: Mill Valley, CA
description: Transition Mill Valley engages the creativity, expertise, and skill-sets of our friends and neighbors in the design of a sustainable, resilient, and mutually-supportive community. We collaborate with other groups to bring about an inspired awakening to the challenges of peak oil and climate change. We recognize the need to build a sustainable, local economy, and to restore the living systems that insure our future. Currently we have an initiating team of 5 people who kicked off Transition in Southern Marin in 2009. This group works with a supporting Hub Group or 30 other highly motivated folks who have interests in the many topical areas of Transition such as localizing food production, energy alternatives, economic alternatives, etc. Transition Mill Valley depends heavily on many partner organizations that work in these key areas. This way the HUB serves as a network source to draw local folks into the discussion and get them connected to the various channels where they can apply their ideas and energy.
tags: Transition Group

name: Monmouth County, NJ
description: We are residents of Monmouth County, NJ forming a Transition Initiative. Our mission is to create local, self sufficient, and sustainable communities. We seek to connect with individuals, organizations and businesses that recognize the need to become more resilient with our sourcing of food, renewable energy, skills and other resources. In response to the global challenges of climate change, economic hardships and dwindling supplies, we seek to raise awareness of our options for creating healthy, sustainable communities and to help implement them. We believe that by getting involved, taking action and re-localizing our communities, we can improve our health, prosperity, and happiness. We welcome your participation!
tags: Transition Group

name: Monterey
description: Communities for Sustainable Monterey County is a local environmental non-profit with a mission to help our communities equitably implement sustainable and regenerative practices to slow and adapt to climate change.
tags: Transition Group, Other food projects, Nature, Social Justice / Just Transition activities

name: Newburyport
description: Overview Transition Newburyport is a local grassroots initiative based on a model developed by Rob Hopkins, a teacher of permaculture and natural building in the UK. A transition initiative is a model for engaging the community in a thoughtful process of planning a local response to the challenges of Climate Change, Natural Resource depletion and Economic Contraction, all of which will bring dramatic changes to life as we know it today. Mission Transition Newburyport is focused on acting together locally to make the transition to a post-fossil-fuel world in a thoughtful and planned way, leading to a more fulfilling, socially connected, sustainable, resilient and self-reliant community.
tags: Transition Group

name: Palo Alto
description: Transition Palo Alto is slowly transforming from an idea into an organization. At this point we know that we want to promote the transition toward a more localized economy, in which the community develops more resilient food, transport, energy, and other systems. As The Transition Handbook argues, that may be the best strategy for dealing with the drastic changes that will be forced by peak oil, climate change, financial instability, and other aspects of living beyond the planet's capacity to support us. It is still very unclear what activities we want to pursue. As first steps we have been organizing film series with discussions (in partnership with Transition Silicon Valley and other organizations), we started a website at http://transitionpaloalto.org/, we studied The Transition Handbook, and several of us attended Transition Training. It's time to discuss what other activities we want to aim for, and what our overall approach should be.
tags: Transition Group

name: Parkside Sustainability Initiative
description: Parkside's slogan, "Small but strong," reflects the heart of our initiative. In a community of approximately 2,300 residents, the initiative began when the Borough Council proposed the elimination of the recycling program. In addition to preserving the recycling program, the group envisions creating a community garden in the small park known as "The Woodlot," promoting the use of solar energy, creating education programs to help citizens live more sustainably, and promote individual gardening, permaculture, individual water reclamation, and other efforts.
tags: Transition Group

name: Phoenixville Area Transition
description: Phoenixville Area Transition works to build community resilience to climate impacts by moving to localize the economy and culture. We do this by connecting people, groups, and the natural world. We formed in Spring of 2018 and have a website in progress at www.pat.community. Our active work groups are Community Supported Food, Films, Inner Transition, and Living Landscapes. One of our missions is to connect like-minded groups in the area and we we have network page on our website with a growing number of these. We've done several Film/Forums at our iconic community theatre, and a hugely successful Summer Solstice Celebration. We appreciate the mentoring we've enjoyed from nearby Transition Town Media, PA, and from Transition US.
tags: Transition Group

name: Quincy Transition Talkoot
description: Connecting the pioneering/miner mindset to the kinship spirit of the Niki'heskum indigenous tradition of all-connectedness in this unique and lovely landscape. There is "Abundance" in sharing and no need to take more than you can reasonably use. Capitalism and inherited wealth promotes the disparity of people and the fear of scarcity. We aim to draw on the strength of people who survived to this Western edge and the Native soul who lived as a function of Place. We want to draw everyone together and respect our individuality.

name: River Song Cohousing Community
description: River Song Cohousing is an intergenerational community of independent households committed to finding purpose and a sense of belonging through working, learning and playing together in a neighborhood designed to make a small and beautiful footprint on the land. Our Website Visit our website (https://riversongcohousing.org/ ) for up-to-date information about our community including: calendar of events, our values statement, cohousing information, site design, information about our members, photos, articles, links, Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ), and more. Short History Cohousing is a type of collaborative housing in which residents actively participate in the design and operation of their community. Oakleigh Meadow Cohousing grew out of the efforts of Joan Connelly and David Adee, who bought a beautifully located property and had the vision to transform this lovely meadow into a cohousing community. The idea grew, starting with a “Getting It Built” workshop in December 2011, and forming an Limited Liability Corporation (LLC) on April 1, 2012, “Oakleigh Meadow, LLC”. While we changed our community name to River Song Cohousing in January, 2020, our legal business name remains Oakleigh Meadow, LLC. All full members are member-managers of the LLC. The Cohousing Concept Dramatic demographic and economic changes have taken place in our society, leaving a mismatch between today’s households and conventional housing. Single-family houses were designed for a 1950s-model family wit...

name: Seattle Reconomy
description: Seattle REconomy is a nonprofit promoting sustainability and community through access to tools, ranging from saws and drills to mowers and pressure washers, as well as classes ranging from gardening to woodworking and home energy improvements. Our resources, classes, and events are available to everyone regardless of place of residence or income. We have over 10,000 tools available to lend.
tags: Transition Group, Community Growing, Building local networks, Local economy or new economy projects, Share, Repair or reuse

name: South Lake Tahoe
description: We are a local climate change group in Lake Tahoe working to make our Tahoe communities as climate friendly and resilient as possible. In addition to our local efforts we connect our members to statewide and national opportunities to effect climate change policy through strategic calls to action. These opportunities include working with national organizations such as the Climate Change Lobby, 350.org, the Climate Reality Project, and other national, regional and local environmental organizations to lobby for progressive climate change policies and to protest and defeat policies and decisions antithetical to achieving climate change emission reduction targets. We are non-partisan and look to build partnerships with any local group or organization that recognizes the threat of climate change and is seeking opportunities to take action.
tags: Transition Group

name: Southwest Virginia Regenerative Ecoregion
description: This initiative enlists a cooperative, multi-platform, inquiry-based approach to fostering an ecoregion's overall health and well-being by translating regenerative "theory" into innovative forms of "practice" among community-coalitions of less than 200 (initial) stakeholders-together, envisioning, designing, and overseeing a project's unfolding.
tags: Transition Group, Community hub or activities, Community visioning / imagination work, Building local networks, Wellbeing or Inner Transition

name: Spokane Manito
description: We've heard about climate change, what we need now is to change for the climate. Spokane Manito is a Transition Group aimed at building communities focused on local climate action and resilience, starting at the neighborhood level. It is also about brainstorming/exploring/trialing/experimenting/prototyping/etc. Drastically reducing our reliance on fossil fuels will require political, economic, and social demand as well as change in how we live. Climate action does not need to mean austerity - it can mean efficiency, equity, and justice.
tags: Transition Group, Community hub or activities, Community visioning / imagination work, Building local networks, Share, Repair or reuse

name: St. Petersburg Transition Initiative
description: St. Petersburg Transition Local Coordinating Hub and its affiliating organizations have thus far the following projects in progress: * Permablitzes to establish gardens on individual properties as well as on community, (shared) sites *Membership in the Sustainable Urban Agriculture Working group of the St. Petersburg Chamber of Commerce * Time Bank *Permaculture Guild- Gaia's Guardians * Bike Share *Planned Neighborhood Initiatives in the Northeast, South, Lakewood, Kenwood and Gulfport areas *Planned creation of a seed bank and tool library, possibly in coordination with our local library system The Heart of Transition On May 22, a local group of ecotherapists, convened by Mary Ann Holtz, LMHC, presented a workshop on inner transition, based upon the pioneering work of Joanna Macy. This group addresses how to support those who feel an emotional impact related to concern for the degredation of our environment,climate and economy, as well as depleted energy resources. Another book study will be offered that will be focused on the book, "Active Hope," also by Joanna Macy. A central premise that is embraced and shared by this group is that, in the words of Joanna Macy, "the pain that people feel about the state of the world is not pathological and should not be labled as such." The ecotherapy group members validate this pain and are committed to being of service to those who are experiencing it, so we can support one another in addressing life's threatening circumstances, in ...
tags: Transition Group

name: Sunnyslope Transition
description: I am interested in being a part of a local Transition Initiative. I'd like to find a local group of like-minded individuals and start. I have contacts with the Sunnyslope Village Alliance (the SVA) and Sunnyslope Historical Society. The SVA is putting together a community garden, and I am planning on making one of the plots a "Children's Garden" over at Mountain View Park. I need people to help out with this. I am interested in gardening, water conservation, trapment and gray water use, solar panels, reducing dependence on consumerism, rethinking waste, microlivestock, food preservation, sourcing everything local, avoiding most plastic, environmental issues and rethinking the economy. I've run a local business for over 8 years. I think a food coop here would be a good idea, although it could start as a buying club. Please contact me.
tags: Transition Group

name: Sustainable Delaware Ohio
description: Sustainable Delaware is a group of residents focused on promoting environmental, social and economic sustainability for the broad Delaware community (city and county) through awareness, advocacy and action. Our current and recent projects include North Central Ohio Pollinator Pathway, the Upcycling Center at Ohio Wesleyan University, and semiannual Repair Cafés. Come and join us!

name: Sustainable Delaware Ohio
description: Sustainable Delaware is a group of residents focused on promoting environmental, social, and economic sustainability for the Delaware, Ohio community through awareness, advocacy and action. Our active projects include pollinator pathways, renewable energy, upcycling, and more!
tags: Transition Group, Energy, Other

name: Sustainable San Leandro
description: Started as a small neighborhood group in the San Francisco Bay Area city of San Leandro, California, we support our community, government, businesses, and schools in a mutual effort toward sustainability. Started with a core group culled from a local book group. we all had a common interest in working toward a sustainable future for our community. Our inception coincidentally coincided roughly with San Leandro's joining the East Bay Green Corridor. While we are not affiliated directly, the East Bay Green Corridor provided a theme of a community-business-education-government alliance. Government involvement included our members attending a number of City Council meetings in 2009 while the San Leandro City Council considered a Climate Action Plan - it was adopted on December 21, 2009. Individually, some of our members have impressively productive vegetable gardens, spawning an annual neighborhood "Green Gathering" - a potluck made from locally grown ingredients.
tags: Transition Group

name: Tallahassee (Transition Initiative)
description: Transition Tallahassee is an upstarting community organization that focuses on three issues: peak oil, climate change, and the economic crisis accompanying peak oil. We work to raise awareness in the Tallahassee community about these three pressing issues, as well as to aid and initiate local alternative infrastructure that will help smooth the transition away from fossil fuel energy, mitigating climate change and easing economic hardship in the process. We strive to create a network of concerned community members and people with skills and knowledge that can help us make this much-needed transition. We work at the community-level, and believe that relocalizing economy and building strong local communities is the best way to meet the coming challenges. We recognize that Tallahassee, being a city with over 180,000 residents, is a very large entity to attempt to transform into a post-oil, tight-knit community, and therefore it is our goal to branch off into smaller neighborhood-based initiatives. We do not discriminate based on ethnicity, sex, gender ID, sexual orientation, class, etc, but rather work to achieve a post-oil community that protects the rights and well-being of all these groups. We always incorporate environmental justice concerns into our efforts. We need your help! We would like to eventually form issue-based subgroups, facilitated by people with knowledge and skills in particular areas. Ideas for subgroups include: Reconnection with Nature | Inner Transition...

name: Transition Berkeley
description: Transition Berkeley was founded in 2011 as the 110th official Transition Initiative in the U.S. Our mission is to bring neighbors and community members together to build a more equitable, regenerative, self-reliant future for Berkeley. We envision a strong, diverse local economy, with greatly reduced dependence on fossil fuels, and a healthy, cooperative, rewarding community life.

name: Transition Bluffton
description: Transition Bluffton’s mission is to be a catalyst for conversation and sustainable change in the Bluffton community. Through the organization of projects of sustainable and cooperative agriculture, responsible transportation, building a resilient economy and the sharing of skills, all through a lens of ecological stewardship and respect of the Earth, we can create a community that is forward looking and based on strong traditions that will stand fast as the future unfolds.
tags: Transition Group

name: Transition Central Arkansas
description: Transition Central Arkansas has had a bit of a slow beginning (we've met since January, 2013), but we're getting off the ground quickly with a very committed team! We'll be presenting the In Transition 2.0 film at Arkansas Earth Day, as well as selling Transition Handbooks, and helping to support local food initiatives, community gardens, and more. Long range plans include a brick-and-mortar food coop, and other projects. Please come and join us!
tags: Transition Group

name: Transition Lake County
description: Transition Lake County (TLC) is a movement and network of citizen-leaders working together to make Lake County more wonderful. Our purpose is to co-create a healthier, happier, more resilient community and local economy. Learn more by visiting us on the web at www.transitionlakecounty.org or contact Nils Palsson at 707.928.0159 or Ed Christwitz at 707.995.0940. TLC on Facebook. TLC on Ning. TLC Home Page: www.transitionlakecounty.org Email seed@transitionlakecounty.org
tags: Transition Group

name: Transition Salt Lake
description: In response to the many challenges facing our community, Transition Salt Lake fosters resilient, viable relationships between people, communities and the natural world which gives us life. The end of the Age of Cheap Oil is rapidly coming upon us. Life will radically change. Transition Salt Lake represents a proactive response to this coming change. Rebuilding local agriculture and food production, localizing energy production, rethinking healthcare and transportation, rediscovering local building materials aiming towards zero energy building, rethinking how we manage waste — all of these build resilience. They also offer the potential of an extraordinary renaissance in Salt Lake - economic, cultural and spiritual. Let’s embrace a world of deeper connections, interdependence, and more fun with less “stuff”. Let’s join together and take steps towards making a nourishing and abundant future a reality. It’s already starting.
tags: Transition Group

name: Transition Town Greater Media
description: Transition Town Greater Media has been an active initiative since 2009. We have an active Biodiversity group that promotes native plants and trees, we've put on hundreds of educational events and potlucks, run a Solarize program and helped initiate others, put on Open Streets events and EcoFests, a Green Sunday Holiday Fair that features local handmade goods, and we work with our local municipal government and county government on several programs, including starting a borough-wide food waste composting program. We have been running a FreeStore since 2014, and a small-business loan and support group since 2019. We helped create and belong to a MidAtlantic regional hub called MAST (MidAtlantic States Transitioners) and have been active with Transition US working groups.

name: Transition Town Jericho
description: Transition Town Jericho (TTJ) is located in Jericho Vermont and started in 2016. It organizes regular public meetings on various resiliency topics, a Kids Garden Contest to promote future gardeners, and Extra Row Project which has farmers plant produce for charity, TTJ providing volunteer labor. Most recently, TTJ helped start a local food hub with its sister city of Underhill, which organizes community meals, workshops, and farmer support, all in the interest of building local food resiliency.

name: Transition Town Port Washington
description: Transition Town Port Washington (TTPW) has a strong legacy of fostering environmental stewardship and promoting sustainable practices within the Port Washington community. Since its founding, TTPW has played an instrumental role in advancing a healthier, more sustainable future by organizing a wide array of impactful initiatives. With a deep commitment to education and community engagement, TTPW hosts citizen science programs that actively involve residents in water testing and environmental monitoring, empowering them to take an active role in protecting local waterways. These efforts not only enhance community awareness, but also directly contribute to safeguarding the health of the region's aquatic ecosystems. TTPW has also held beach cleanups and spearheaded the installation of a beach cleanup station within a popular local beachfront preserve with the hopes of encouraging the public to remove waterfront debris on a daily basis. Through initiatives like these and a variety of other programs that focus on climate action, waste reduction, and youth engagement, TTPW is helping to create a ripple effect of positive change within the community. Collaboration is at the heart of TTPW's approach, and it partners with local organizations such as the Port Washington Public Library, the Science Museum of Long Island, and Landmark Theater to bring environmental topics to the forefront through dynamic educational programming. name: Transition Whatcom
description: Nestled in the northwest corner of Washington state, Transition Whatcom's VISION is resilient and more self-reliant communities throughout Whatcom County with a local food supply, sustainable energy sources, a healthy local economy, and a growing sense of vitality and community well-being. The MISSION of Transition Whatcom is: 1) To explore and then follow pathways of practical actions that will reduce our carbon emissions and dependence on fossil fuels. 2) To rebuild our community's resilience, that is, its ability to withstand shocks from the outside, through being more self-reliant in areas such as food, energy, health care, jobs and economics. 3) To inspire and support the communities and neighborhoods of Whatcom County as they work towards resilience at these local levels.

name: Two Peaks Transition
description: Two Peaks Transition is a Colorado nonprofit corporation formed to inform, inspire, promote and support local residents in the move toward a local, resilient and sustainable future. Two Peaks Transition was born from the efforts of Drs. Heloise and Walter Lynn who presented the Transition concepts to audiences in La Veta, Walsenburg, Trinidad, and Gardner. Both Heloise and Walter have PhD's in geophysics from Stanford University and have each had a distinguished career as a geophysicist. Their inspiring presentations led almost immediately to formation of the La Veta Mullers which grew into Two Peaks Transition. Heloise and Walter continue to present inspiring educational programs to raise awareness regarding the issues of peak oil, climate change and economic instability. Two Peaks Transition is in the process of creating and implementing an energy descent plan to assist communities in the Spanish Peaks area on the path to being resilient and localized in food, energy, health care, emergency services, economy, education, the arts and transportation.
tags: Transition Group

name: WE Network
description: We rebuild ghost towns to house and employ low-income and homeless while also providing them a new career. RBE Co-op Village & Garden We are rebuilding our ghost town of Clubb, MO. (if you could even call it that because there's nothing left but a few wells and some ruins.) We are looking for any craftsmen or artisans that would like to run any of the shops that we are creating. A dairy, a butcher, a soap maker, a blacksmith, etc any Artisans really we will help build a small shop if they join our co-op we are doing it silver dollar city style but only open to the public on weekends (most of our business will be done online) To provide the needed raw materials, we are starting EcoJunk and a community garden ( we are kind of doing Garden of Eden Style- rewilding the vegetables and fruits and even our livestock grown in a way to easily be regenerative and much, much less work for us) WE is a recipient owned non-profit, that researches sustainable community development. WE groups together homeless & low-income families to help each other while finding a smarter path for our future https://wanderersend.org
tags: Transition Group

name: West Chester Area Transition
description: An outgrowth of the West Chester Green Team which addresses issues of energy, climate, pesticides, and plastics, a transition initiative has emerged with its focus on community gardening, soil restoration through meadows and reduction in single use plastics. A team of 6 met online for our first brainstorming meeting. Additional recommendations emerged from about a dozen Green Team members. We have received advice and support from Transition Towns-Phoenixville and Media as well as the US national office. We are working in collaboration with West Chester University, Chester County Environmental Alliance, Sierra Club Ready for 100, West Chester Co-operative, Buy Nothing, West Chester and local governments.
tags: Transition Group

name: Woburn Local
description: So far, we're a group on SCI's Woburn Web site. Invitation via blogs on that site by David Eggleton in the summer of 2008. David blogs at woburnite.com, has attended the 2-day Training for Transition (11/08) and has attained permaculture design certification (4/10).
tags: Transition Group

name: Woodstock NY Transition
description: In 2012, Woodstock, NY became the 115th official Transition Town in the US. Our little town is part of the Transition Movement that’s currently active all over this planet! Over the years, our working groups have ebbed and flowed. Some formed and dissolved when their task was complete such as the 10% Challenge, the Solarize Campaign, and the Drinking Water Source Protection. We are in the midst of our 11th Annual Film and Discussion Series and it is going strong. Repair Café Woodstock formed in 2017 and after the interruption of COVID is once again gathering momentum. The Working Group Support group recently dissolved and re-formed as Deep Listening Group, still meeting weekly since 2013. The Energy Group is quietly working in the background with the town government to seek funding for a ground-source district energy scoping study. Woodstock Pollinator Pathway is buzzing along. After many years of vibrant monthly gatherings, the Garden Share Group is currently inactive. Most of our efforts involve collaboration with other non-profits, faith-based groups, and/or municipal entities. Woodstock Transition has an excellent reputation in the community.
tags: Transition Group

name: Ypsilanti   (Transition Ypsilanti)
description: We are a "still mulling it over" group, although we are evolving. There have been a variety of community workshops being held on a regular basis on various topics related to transition, as well as interaction with other transition members from around the state. Ypsilanti is a walkable city that places a strong emphasis on valuing historic areas, and park spaces. We have community growing and gardening thru Growing Hope and an active farmer's market as well as some budding urban farmers. Small projects such as citizens organizing to put a solar panel on city hall and the local food coop are significant in promoting an awareness of renewable energy as important to the city.
tags: Transition Group
